title: Ancistrum trifidum
taxonomy: Ancistrum trifidum is a species of armored catfish, belonging to the family Loricariidae. The Loricariidae family is a group of catfish commonly known as armored catfish due to the presence of bony plates on their bodies. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, but Ancistrum trifidum's classification at the subfamily level is not well-defined. Ancistrum trifidum is native to South America, and its classification is part of a broader study of the diverse catfish species found in this region. Further research is needed to determine the exact classification of Ancistrum trifidum.
